user_query:
I’m a fan of alternative and folk music and started this tiny Python tool after a blogger’s recommendations led me to Angus & Julia Stone. Please do a focused code review and refactor of the included script, then produce ranked track outputs and concise communications. Use only the provided files and the Python standard library.

Inputs to use (do not modify these):
- Code: src/playlist_tool.py
- Data: input/songs.csv
- Config: input/config.yaml

Your tasks and required outputs:
1) Code review and refactor
- Review src/playlist_tool.py and identify concrete issues (e.g., resource handling, duplication, naming, cohesion, testability). Refactor into a clean, modular, and testable script saved to refactor/playlist_tool_refactored.py.
- The refactored script must provide a CLI:
  python refactor/playlist_tool_refactored.py --input input/songs.csv --config input/config.yaml --out outputs/top_folk_alt.csv
- Implement deterministic ranking driven by config values:
  • Filter rows whose genre contains any of the tokens in config.genres_filter (case-insensitive substring match).
  • Compute score = rating_weight * (rating/5.0) + plays_weight * (plays / max_plays_in_filtered_set).
  • Sort by score descending and write up to top_n rows (or all if fewer) to outputs/top_folk_alt.csv with columns: id,title,artist,genre,rating,plays,score.

2) Compose a concise status update report
- Create outputs/status_update.md (<=300 words) including:
  • Summary (1–2 sentences) of what changed and why.
  • Changes made: a short, bulleted list.
  • Metrics: lines of code and function count before vs after (measured from the original src/playlist_tool.py and your refactor/playlist_tool_refactored.py).
  • Top 5 picks: list title, artist, and score from the first five rows of outputs/top_folk_alt.csv.

3) Draft an email to the blogger
- Create outputs/email_to_blogger.txt as a plain-text email with fields:
  To: <placeholder email>
  Subject: Brief update on my folk/alternative playlist tool
  Body: A warm, human message (3–6 sentences) that:
    • Thanks them for leading you to Angus & Julia Stone.
    • Summarizes the improvements you made.
    • Mentions the single highest-ranked Angus & Julia Stone track found in outputs/top_folk_alt.csv (by scanning the file for artist == "Angus & Julia Stone").

Constraints and notes:
- Do not require external packages or Internet access.
- Make sure outputs/top_folk_alt.csv deterministically reflects input/songs.csv and input/config.yaml per the rules above.
- Keep file paths and output formats exactly as specified so I can verify them locally.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
